LRT2301.module_title: Autonomous Mobile Systems on Planetary Surfaces
LRT2301.module_title_en: Autonomous Mobile Systems on Planetary Surfaces
LRT2301.module_content_en: Planetary mobility, hazard-aware planning, terrain interaction, slip estimation, and autonomy under sparse communication windows.
LRT2301.module_outcome_en: Students can reason about rover autonomy stacks and connect mobility constraints to real planetary missions.
LRT2301.module_precondition_en: Basic control theory, robotics foundations, and scientific programming.

2026s.course_name_en: Autonomous Mobile Systems on Planetary Surfaces

LRT2314.module_content_en: Docking dynamics, orbital manipulation, contact-aware control, and simulation of robotic servicing missions.
LRT2314.module_outcome_en: Students can evaluate how orbital robotics differs from terrestrial autonomy and map the skill overlap.
LRT2314.module_precondition_en: Orbital mechanics, dynamics, and linear control.

2026s.course_name_en: Orbital Robotic Systems: Mechanics and Control

CIT4128.module_content_en: Perception, localization, visual odometry, and robust navigation for robots in unstructured environments.
CIT4128.module_outcome_en: Students gain concrete autonomy techniques directly relevant to rover perception and navigation.
CIT4128.module_precondition_en: Linear algebra, robotics basics, and Python or C++.

org.org_name_en: Institute of Robotics and Machine Intelligence
